title: Digital Connection vs. Mental Well-being
content: Title slide introducing a presentation on the relationship between digital connection and mental well-being. Presented by Li Wei, a Year 2 Sociology student, for the Global Studies 201 course.

title: The Ubiquitous Landscape
content: This slide describes Gen Z as digital natives who experience life through an online lens. It highlights heavy daily usage of platforms like YouTube, Instagram, and Snapchat, framing constant connectivity as an indispensable part of modern life.
key_claims: Gen Z are recognized as Digital Natives, experiencing life through an online lens
Constant connectivity is a fundamental, indispensable reality in the modern era

title: Usage and Anxiety Statistics
content: This slide presents a bar chart comparing social media usage levels with related mental health outcomes, citing a 2025 Mental Health Statistics Report. It links high usage rates to reported anxiety/sadness and increased depression risk.
key_claims: 87% of users have usage exceeding 2 hours/day

title: Psychological Manifestations
content: This slide outlines three key psychological effects of social media use: FOMO, social comparison, and cyberbullying. Each is presented as a distinct card with an icon and brief description of its impact on mental health.
key_claims: FOMO Effect: fear of missing out creates significant stress and anxiety regarding social inclusion
Comparison: constant exposure to curated feeds leads to negative self-image and low self-esteem
Cyberbullying: nearly 87% of teens have experienced some form of online harassment or negative interactions

title: Strategic Mitigation Efforts
content: This slide proposes three strategies to address the negative mental health impacts of digital connectivity: digital detox, increased awareness, and platform responsibility. Each strategy is presented with an icon and brief explanation.
key_claims: Digital Detox: implementing time limits is a crucial strategy for emotional balance
Awareness: education regarding platform algorithms is essentially significant
Platform Responsibility: companies should prioritize user well-being as a primary imperative
